title: ocotea tabascensis
intro: Ocotea tabascensis, also known as the Mexican sassafras, is a plant species native to Mexico and Central America, belonging to the Lauraceae family. Its bark and roots contain a compound called safrole, which has been used for centuries in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including analgesic, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic effects. The health benefits of Ocotea tabascensis include relief from arthritis, muscle pain, and digestive issues, as well as its potential to reduce stress and promote relaxation. Herbal preparations of Ocotea tabascensis typically involve infusions or decoctions of the bark or roots, which can be consumed as teas or used in topical applications for wound care and skin issues.
